name: Lir
description: Lir, whose name translates to 'the Sea', is a prominent figure in Irish mythology, primarily known as the god of the sea. While not always depicted as a king of the gods like Poseidon or Neptune, he holds significant power and influence over the marine realm. He is often associated with the sea's vastness, its unpredictable nature, and its inherent beauty. Lir's lineage is sometimes debated, but he is generally considered a member of the Tuatha Dé Danann, the divine race who ruled Ireland before the arrival of humans. He is perhaps best known for the tragic tale of his children, Fionnuala, Aodh, Fiachra, and Conn, who were transformed into swans by his jealous wife, Aoife, and forced to endure 900 years of suffering before being restored to their human forms upon hearing the sound of a Christian bell. This story highlights the themes of betrayal, sorrow, and eventual redemption that are central to Irish folklore. Lir's power extends beyond simply controlling the waves; he embodies the sea's spirit and represents its connection to the land and the people. He is a figure of both awe and respect, embodying the untamed force of nature and the enduring power of love and loss. He's sometimes associated with horses and chariots due to their connection with water and rapid movement, further emphasizing his dominion over the vast and powerful ocean. Lir's influence permeates many aspects of Irish culture, serving as a reminder of the sea's integral role in their history and identity.
subtitle: God of the Sea, Father of Sorrows

keyMyths.title: The Children of Lir
keyMyths.description: Aoife, Lir's jealous wife, transforms his children into swans who endure 900 years of suffering. They are eventually restored to their human forms upon hearing a Christian bell.

keyMyths.title: Lir's Lineage
keyMyths.description: The exact lineage of Lir is debated, but he is generally considered a member of the Tuatha Dé Danann. This places him among the divine race who ruled Ireland before the arrival of humans.

keyMyths.title: Lir and Manannán
keyMyths.description: Lir is often associated with Manannán mac Lir, another significant sea deity in Irish mythology. Their relationship can be seen as complementary, representing different aspects of the sea's power and influence.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
